TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: aitsu uri pasemalaw aken tua sikazepzepan nua maretseketsekeɫ a mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: aiʦu uri pasəmaɭaw akən tua sikazəpzəpan nua marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ a mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: aicu uri pasemalaw aken tua sikazepzepan nua marecekecekelj a mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: aiʦu uri pasəmaɭaw akən tua sikazəpzəpan nua marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ a mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: Now I am going to tell you about the discussions of chiefly families who are planning to arrange a marriage.

S.FORM: aitsu nu izua uri marepaukuukuz nua mamazangiɫan, azua sauqaɫay mangtjez a sema zua itua savavayan; sa patsun tazua sauzayan a uri sinisusu tua zua vavayan, sa kazepzep tuki marasudj tuki ini.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu izua uri marəpaukuukuz nua mamazaŋiʎan, azua sauqaʎaj maŋcəz a səma zua itua savavajan; sa paʦun tazua sauzajan a uri sinisusu tua zua vavajan, sa kazəpzəp tuki marasuɉ tuki ini.
S.FORM: aicu nu izua uri marepaukuukuz nua mamazangiljan, azua sauqaljay mangtjez a sema zua itua savavayan; sa pacun tazua sauzayan a uri sinisusu tua zua vavayan, sa kazepzep tuki marasudj tuki ini.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu izua uri marəpaukuukuz nua mamazaŋiʎan, azua sauqaʎaj maŋʦəz a səma zua itua savavajan; sa paʦun tazua sauzajan a uri sinisusu tua zua vavajan, sa kazəpzəp tuki marasuɉ tuki ini.
S.TRANSL: When chiefly families are going to have a betrothal the man’s family come to the woman’s place; and they (the bride’s family) look at the possessions which are to be given as a bride price for the woman, and they discuss whether they can agree or not.

S.FORM: nu inika marasudj magagulagula tua maretseketsekeɫ.
S.PHON: nu inika marasuɉ magaguɭaguɭa tua marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ.
S.FORM: nu inika marasudj magagulagula tua marecekecekelj.
S.PHON: nu inika marasuɉ magaguɭaguɭa tua marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ.
S.TRANSL: If they don’t agree the marriage cannot go forward.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu marasudj tjara marepaukuukuz anga tiamadju.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu marasuɉ cara marəpaukuukuz aŋa tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: ljakua nu marasudj tjara marepaukuukuz anga tiamadju.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu marasuɉ ʦara marəpaukuukuz aŋa tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: But if they agree they certainly are betrothed.

S.FORM: qau aitsu uri qivu anga ken tua kapatatevelavela niamadju.
S.PHON: qau aiʦu uri qivu aŋa kən tua kapatatəvəɭavəɭa niamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau aicu uri qivu anga ken tua kapatatevelavela niamadju.
S.PHON: qau aiʦu uri qivu aŋa kən tua kapatatəvəɭavəɭa niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: So now I’ll tell you the dialogue between them.

S.FORM: maitutsu a savavayan: pai aitsu a timun a nia kaka a nia vetjek, uri putakalanan aken.
S.PHON: maituʦu a savavajan: pai aiʦu a timun a nia kaka a nia vəcək, uri putakaɭanan akən.
S.FORM: maitucu a savavayan: pai aicu a timun a nia kaka a nia vetjek, uri putakalanan aken.
S.PHON: maituʦu a savavajan: pai aiʦu a timun a nia kaka a nia vəʦək, uri putakaɭanan akən.
S.TRANSL: This is what the woman’s side say: Now our brothers and sisters I have something to say.

S.FORM: aku ika tjen a maleva, aku ika tjen a seman azua tua mareka tja kaka tua mareka tja sikataqaɫan a nakemasi tjuqaɫa.
S.PHON: aku ika cən a maɭəva, aku ika cən a səman azua tua marəka ca kaka tua marəka ca sikataqaʎan a nakəmasi cuqaʎa.
S.FORM: aku ika tjen a maleva, aku ika tjen a seman azua tua mareka tja kaka tua mareka tja sikataqaljan a nakemasi tjuqalja.
S.PHON: aku ika ʦən a maɭəva, aku ika ʦən a səman azua tua marəka ʦa kaka tua marəka ʦa sikataqaʎan a nakəmasi ʦuqaʎa.
S.TRANSL: Why shouldn’t we be glad and praise one another as siblings and fellow-tribespeople from elsewhere?

S.FORM: maɫimaɫi a nasaɫinga mun a nakiɫivak mun nalitjengits(?) mun kaumaya tua nu aɫak tua nu vuvu.
S.PHON: maʎimaʎi a nasaʎiŋa mun a nakiʎivak mun naɭicəŋiʦ(ʔ) mun kaumaja tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu.
S.FORM: maljimalji a nasaljinga mun a nakiljivak mun nalitjengic(') mun kaumaya tua nu aljak tua nu vuvu.
S.PHON: maʎimaʎi a nasaʎiŋa mun a nakiʎivak mun naɭiʦəŋiʦ(ʔ) mun kaumaja tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu.
S.TRANSL: We are grateful that you want, love and perhaps(?) your child or grandchild.

S.FORM: ɫakua pai aitsu a nu sinipaqayam aitsu a nu kinuletj a nu linanaw tu kiɫivak tua nu aɫak tua nu vuvu, aku inika uri tsengtseng, aku inika uri nanguaq a tja sipalungulungu nua mareka kaka a marevetjevetjek, aya sa ku kininemneman.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai aiʦu a nu sinipaqajam aiʦu a nu kinuɭəc a nu ɭinanaw tu kiʎivak tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu, aku inika uri ʦəŋʦəŋ, aku inika uri naŋuaq a ca sipaɭuŋuɭuŋu nua marəka kaka a marəvəcəvəcək, aja sa ku kininəmnəman.
S.FORM: ljakua pai aicu a nu sinipaqayam aicu a nu kinuletj a nu linanaw tu kiljivak tua nu aljak tua nu vuvu, aku inika uri cengceng, aku inika uri nanguaq a tja sipalungulungu nua mareka kaka a marevetjevetjek, aya sa ku kininemneman.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai aiʦu a nu sinipaqajam aiʦu a nu kinuɭəʦ a nu ɭinanaw tu kiʎivak tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu, aku inika uri ʦəŋʦəŋ, aku inika uri naŋuaq a ʦa sipaɭuŋuɭuŋu nua marəka kaka a marəvəʦəvəʦək, aja sa ku kininəmnəman.
S.TRANSL: But what you have set out, what you have provided as an expression(?) of your love for your child or grandchild, why isn’t it suitable, why isn’t it up to what we expect(?) of our brothers and sisters?, is what I am thinking.

S.FORM: sauqaɫay: ui tjara maitazua, uɫa namaya aya men.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: ui cara maitazua, uʎa namaja aja mən.
S.FORM: sauqaljay: ui tjara maitazua, ulja namaya aya men.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: ui ʦara maitazua, uʎa namaja aja mən.
S.TRANSL: Man’s side: Yes, that’s true. We want the same.

S.FORM: nia kinɫang tu inika napenapadjulu, tu inika uri nu siselaing a nia vusam a nia tjaɫayan.
S.PHON: nia kinʎaŋ tu inika napənapaɉuɭu, tu inika uri nu sisəɭaiŋ a nia vusam a nia caʎajan.
S.FORM: nia kinljang tu inika napenapadjulu, tu inika uri nu siselaing a nia vusam a nia tjaljayan.
S.PHON: nia kinʎaŋ tu inika napənapaɉuɭu, tu inika uri nu sisəɭaiŋ a nia vusam a nia ʦaʎajan.
S.TRANSL: We know that it looks as if it’s not properly prepared, that it wouldn’t be acceptable to you our original roots.

S.FORM: ɫakua qau saka nu aya itjen.
S.PHON: ʎakua qau saka nu aja icən.
S.FORM: ljakua qau saka nu aya itjen.
S.PHON: ʎakua qau saka nu aja iʦən.
S.TRANSL: But then what are we to do?

S.FORM: pai maɫi ki tsadja i inuan a nu, a kinitjuayan a kiniɫaɫaungan nua nu aɫak nua nu vuvu uta, nu keɫangen uta tu nasemalimsim, nu keɫangen uta tu napenapeqauqaung uta aravats; saka kiɫaɫava mun saka pakadjele mun a maya numa.
S.PHON: pai maʎi ki ʦaɉa i inuan a nu, a kinicuajan a kiniʎaʎauŋan nua nu aʎak nua nu vuvu uta, nu kəʎaŋən uta tu nasəmaɭimsim, nu kəʎaŋən uta tu napənapəqauqauŋ uta aravaʦ; saka kiʎaʎava mun saka pakaɉəɭə mun a maja numa.
S.FORM: pai malji ki cadja i inuan a nu, a kinitjuayan a kiniljaljaungan nua nu aljak nua nu vuvu uta, nu keljangen uta tu nasemalimsim, nu keljangen uta tu napenapeqauqaung uta aravac; saka kiljaljava mun saka pakadjele mun a maya numa.
S.PHON: pai maʎi ki ʦaɉa i inuan a nu, a kiniʦuajan a kiniʎaʎauŋan nua nu aʎak nua nu vuvu uta, nu kəʎaŋən uta tu nasəmaɭimsim, nu kəʎaŋən uta tu napənapəqauqauŋ uta aravaʦ; saka kiʎaʎava mun saka pakaɉəɭə mun a maja numa.
S.TRANSL: It would be different if the possessions and abodes of your children and grandchildren were a long way away. You know too that we are badly off, you know that we are very pitiable too; and you were waiting for us and you seem to belittle(?) us.

S.FORM: qau mere anemanga men uta a nu aɫak a nu vuvu; nu aitutsuin uta aravats a paqaung aravats.
S.PHON: qau mərə anəmaŋa mən uta a nu aʎak a nu vuvu; nu aituʦuin uta aravaʦ a paqauŋ aravaʦ.
S.FORM: qau mere anemanga men uta a nu aljak a nu vuvu; nu aitucuin uta aravac a paqaung aravac.
S.PHON: qau mərə anəmaŋa mən uta a nu aʎak a nu vuvu; nu aituʦuin uta aravaʦ a paqauŋ aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: So what are we to become, we your children and grandchildren? You treat us like this and cause us a lot of pain.

S.FORM: savavayan: ui ma, tjara maitazua.
S.PHON: savavajan: ui ma, cara maitazua.
S.FORM: savavayan: ui ma, tjara maitazua.
S.PHON: savavajan: ui ma, ʦara maitazua.
S.TRANSL: Woman’s side: Well yes, it’s like that.

S.FORM: nia keɫangen a maitazua ɫakua, pai aitsu a nu varung uta a nu kininemneman uta nia, a mareka nia kaka sauqaɫay, inika nu sinipaquliqulidan aravats kaumaya ta nu kininemneman.
S.PHON: nia kəʎaŋən a maitazua ʎakua, pai aiʦu a nu varuŋ uta a nu kininəmnəman uta nia, a marəka nia kaka sauqaʎaj, inika nu sinipaquɭiquɭidan aravaʦ kaumaja ta nu kininəmnəman.
S.FORM: nia keljangen a maitazua ljakua, pai aicu a nu varung uta a nu kininemneman uta nia, a mareka nia kaka sauqaljay, inika nu sinipaquliqulidan aravac kaumaya ta nu kininemneman.
S.PHON: nia kəʎaŋən a maitazua ʎakua, pai aiʦu a nu varuŋ uta a nu kininəmnəman uta nia, a marəka nia kaka sauqaʎaj, inika nu sinipaquɭiquɭidan aravaʦ kaumaja ta nu kininəmnəman.
S.TRANSL: We know it’s like that; but, brothers and sisters of the man’s side, your hearts and thoughts, you have not been truly honest in your thoughts.

S.FORM: pai aitsu nu kana nu nalemanaw(?) mun, nuka nu pinaquliqulidanen a nu kininemneman, uzi ki kana nu pinarumaya, ɫakua ayatua namatsaquan mun a kinemnem, tutsu anga maɫian anga tja kakudan, inika tjen a kematsukatsu tua sitiavan tua sikatjeluan a tja kakudan.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu nu kana nu naɭəmanaw(ʔ) mun, nuka nu pinaquɭiquɭidanən a nu kininəmnəman, uzi ki kana nu pinarumaja, ʎakua ajatua namaʦaquan mun a kinəmnəm, tuʦu aŋa maʎian aŋa ca kakudan, inika cən a kəmaʦukaʦu tua sitiavan tua sikacəɭuan a ca kakudan.
S.FORM: pai aicu nu kana nu nalemanaw(') mun, nuka nu pinaquliqulidanen a nu kininemneman, uzi ki kana nu pinarumaya, ljakua ayatua namacaquan mun a kinemnem, tucu anga maljian anga tja kakudan, inika tjen a kemacukacu tua sitiavan tua sikatjeluan a tja kakudan.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu nu kana nu naɭəmanaw(ʔ) mun, nuka nu pinaquɭiquɭidanən a nu kininəmnəman, uzi ki kana nu pinarumaja, ʎakua ajatua namaʦaquan mun a kinəmnəm, tuʦu aŋa maʎian aŋa ʦa kakudan, inika ʦən a kəmaʦukaʦu tua sitiavan tua sikaʦəɭuan a ʦa kakudan.
S.TRANSL: Well if you had ?, if you had been honest in your thinking, you would have ?. But because you are clever at thinking, (you say) now the customs have changed, we don’t keep on the customs of yesterday and the day before.

S.FORM: aitsu a izuanga tja kivadaqan a ḍipung a imaza i kungkuan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a izuaŋa ca kivadaqan a ɖipuŋ a imaza i kuŋkuan.
S.FORM: aicu a izuanga tja kivadaqan a dripung a imaza i kungkuan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a izuaŋa ʦa kivadaqan a ɖipuŋ a imaza i kuŋkuan.
S.TRANSL: Now there are the Japanese here in the police station to ask.

S.FORM: ki tja paligiligiɫi anga, ki tja katsaqu-i anga a qivu tiamadju, sa tje palaluqeluqemi, aya nu varung.
S.PHON: ki ca paɭigiɭigiʎi aŋa, ki ca kaʦaqu-i aŋa a qivu tiamaɉu, sa cə paɭaɭuqəɭuqəmi, aja nu varuŋ.
S.FORM: ki tja paligiligilji anga, ki tja kacaqu-i anga a qivu tiamadju, sa tje palaluqeluqemi, aya nu varung.
S.PHON: ki ʦa paɭigiɭigiʎi aŋa, ki ʦa kaʦaqu-i aŋa a qivu tiamaɉu, sa ʦə paɭaɭuqəɭuqəmi, aja nu varuŋ.
S.TRANSL: We’ll deceive them, we’ll talk cleverly to them and we’ll get the better of them, is what you’re saying in your hearts.

S.FORM: ɫakua pai aitsu a nu vuvu aitsu a nu aɫak palisi a nu katsaquin, palisi a nu kaligiɫen a qivu.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai aiʦu a nu vuvu aiʦu a nu aʎak paɭisi a nu kaʦaquin, paɭisi a nu kaɭigiʎən a qivu.
S.FORM: ljakua pai aicu a nu vuvu aicu a nu aljak palisi a nu kacaquin, palisi a nu kaligiljen a qivu.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai aiʦu a nu vuvu aiʦu a nu aʎak paɭisi a nu kaʦaquin, paɭisi a nu kaɭigiʎən a qivu.
S.TRANSL: But it’s taboo to fool your grandchild or child, it’s taboo to deceive her in words.

S.FORM: nu namaitazua a nu varung, neka, ika tjen a sa malalaing angata.
S.PHON: nu namaitazua a nu varuŋ, nəka, ika cən a sa maɭaɭaiŋ aŋata.
S.FORM: nu namaitazua a nu varung, neka, ika tjen a sa malalaing angata.
S.PHON: nu namaitazua a nu varuŋ, nəka, ika ʦən a sa maɭaɭaiŋ aŋata.
S.TRANSL: If that’s the way your hearts are, that’s it, we certainly won’t agree.

S.FORM: sauqaɫay: paḍia mun paḍia mun a nia vusam a mareka nia kaka.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: paɖia mun paɖia mun a nia vusam a marəka nia kaka.
S.FORM: sauqaljay: padria mun padria mun a nia vusam a mareka nia kaka.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: paɖia mun paɖia mun a nia vusam a marəka nia kaka.
S.TRANSL: Man’s side: You are quite right, quite right, brothers and sisters who are our roots.

S.FORM: ɫakua pai mereanemanga uta nu aɫak a nu kaka.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai mərəanəmaŋa uta nu aʎak a nu kaka.
S.FORM: ljakua pai mereanemanga uta nu aljak a nu kaka.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai mərəanəmaŋa uta nu aʎak a nu kaka.
S.TRANSL: But what are we to become, your children and your brothers and sisters?

S.FORM: pai titjen a tsautsau anga kasaɫinga-i kipakimi tua mana tsekeɫ nua tja aɫak, nu aya itjen a timaimanga, tjara napapuɫat itjen tjara nakinemnem itjen tua mana uri napenapadjulu, nu mateveteveɫ uta tjara liaw aravats a kai, tjara ɫakua, qau saka nema tja alapen, pakasinu itjen nu masa uraman nu masa sinalimsiman.
S.PHON: pai ticən a ʦauʦau aŋa kasaʎiŋa-i kipakimi tua mana ʦəkəʎ nua ca aʎak, nu aja icən a timaimaŋa, cara napapuʎat icən cara nakinəmnəm icən tua mana uri napənapaɉuɭu, nu matəvətəvəʎ uta cara ɭiaw aravaʦ a kai, cara ʎakua, qau saka nəma ca aɭapən, pakasinu icən nu masa uraman nu masa sinaɭimsiman.
S.FORM: pai titjen a caucau anga kasaljinga-i kipakimi tua mana cekelj nua tja aljak, nu aya itjen a timaimanga, tjara napapuljat itjen tjara nakinemnem itjen tua mana uri napenapadjulu, nu matevetevelj uta tjara liaw aravac a kai, tjara ljakua, qau saka nema tja alapen, pakasinu itjen nu masa uraman nu masa sinalimsiman.
S.PHON: pai tiʦən a ʦauʦau aŋa kasaʎiŋa-i kipakimi tua mana ʦəkəʎ nua ʦa aʎak, nu aja iʦən a timaimaŋa, ʦara napapuʎat iʦən ʦara nakinəmnəm iʦən tua mana uri napənapaɉuɭu, nu matəvətəvəʎ uta ʦara ɭiaw aravaʦ a kai, ʦara ʎakua, qau saka nəma ʦa aɭapən, pakasinu iʦən nu masa uraman nu masa sinaɭimsiman.
S.TRANSL: All of us human beings when anyone of us says that we want and are looking for a spouse for our child, we certainly do our best, we certainly think of what will be proper provision. But what are we to take, what way are we to take because of our need and our pitiable state?

S.FORM: pai aitsu a nu aɫak a nu vuvu mana sika namaitutsu, aitsu kinamiaminan anga pinapuɫatan anga.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu a nu aʎak a nu vuvu mana sika namaituʦu, aiʦu kinamiaminan aŋa pinapuʎatan aŋa.
S.FORM: pai aicu a nu aljak a nu vuvu mana sika namaitucu, aicu kinamiaminan anga pinapuljatan anga.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu a nu aʎak a nu vuvu mana sika namaituʦu, aiʦu kinamiaminan aŋa pinapuʎatan aŋa.
S.TRANSL: And that’s the way it is with your child or grandchild. We have got to the end of everything, we have done everything for him.

S.FORM: ɫakua tjara nu pakadjelein.
S.PHON: ʎakua cara nu pakaɉəɭəin.
S.FORM: ljakua tjara nu pakadjelein.
S.PHON: ʎakua ʦara nu pakaɉəɭəin.
S.TRANSL: But you just despise (?) it.

S.FORM: ɫakua qadjaw nimun uta.
S.PHON: ʎakua qaɉaw nimun uta.
S.FORM: ljakua qadjaw nimun uta.
S.PHON: ʎakua qaɉaw nimun uta.
S.TRANSL: But it’s up to you,

S.FORM: nu ika saqetju a nu varung, nu ika mun a saleɫseɫ uta tua nu aɫak, timun a mareka a tia kama a tia kina.
S.PHON: nu ika saqəcu a nu varuŋ, nu ika mun a saɭəʎsəʎ uta tua nu aʎak, timun a marəka a tia kama a tia kina.
S.FORM: nu ika saqetju a nu varung, nu ika mun a saleljselj uta tua nu aljak, timun a mareka a tia kama a tia kina.
S.PHON: nu ika saqəʦu a nu varuŋ, nu ika mun a saɭəʎsəʎ uta tua nu aʎak, timun a marəka a tia kama a tia kina.
S.TRANSL: if your hearts aren’t sad, if you don’t have pity on your child, our fathers and mothers.

S.FORM: savavayan: maitazua ɫakua uri mezangal sa ku kai.
S.PHON: savavajan: maitazua ʎakua uri məzaŋaɭ sa ku kai.
S.FORM: savavayan: maitazua ljakua uri mezangal sa ku kai.
S.PHON: savavajan: maitazua ʎakua uri məzaŋaɭ sa ku kai.
S.TRANSL: Woman’s side: That’s so; but I’m going to go on speaking.

S.FORM: pai kana sa kudanga kana kamatsulen tua sitsuayan tua sitiavan tua sikatjeluan.
S.PHON: pai kana sa kudaŋa kana kamaʦuɭən tua siʦuajan tua sitiavan tua sikacəɭuan.
S.FORM: pai kana sa kudanga kana kamaculen tua sicuayan tua sitiavan tua sikatjeluan.
S.PHON: pai kana sa kudaŋa kana kamaʦuɭən tua siʦuajan tua sitiavan tua sikaʦəɭuan.
S.TRANSL: (?) How would it be if we followed the customs of long ago, of yesterday and the day before.

S.FORM: pai katiaw kasikatjelu na tja pinakakaizuazua a tja pinakakudakudanga nu mareka saɫingaɫinga itjen a mareka kaka a marevetjevetjek a tjara kavusavusaman.
S.PHON: pai katiaw kasikacəɭu na ca pinakakaizuazua a ca pinakakudakudaŋa nu marəka saʎiŋaʎiŋa icən a marəka kaka a marəvəcəvəcək a cara kavusavusaman.
S.FORM: pai katiaw kasikatjelu na tja pinakakaizuazua a tja pinakakudakudanga nu mareka saljingaljinga itjen a mareka kaka a marevetjevetjek a tjara kavusavusaman.
S.PHON: pai katiaw kasikaʦəɭu na ʦa pinakakaizuazua a ʦa pinakakudakudaŋa nu marəka saʎiŋaʎiŋa iʦən a marəka kaka a marəvəʦəvəʦək a ʦara kavusavusaman.
S.TRANSL: Well yesterday and the day before (?) there were all sorts of things we had to give and do if we wanted one another, we brothers and sisters of the real chiefly families.

S.FORM: ɫakua pai tutsu malumay anga, izuanga tja kivadaqan izua pazazekazekatj tjanuitjen numa.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai tuʦu maɭumaj aŋa, izuaŋa ca kivadaqan izua pazazəkazəkac canuicən numa.
S.FORM: ljakua pai tucu malumay anga, izuanga tja kivadaqan izua pazazekazekatj tjanuitjen numa.
S.PHON: ʎakua pai tuʦu maɭumaj aŋa, izuaŋa ʦa kivadaqan izua pazazəkazəkaʦ ʦanuiʦən numa.
S.TRANSL: But now we have gone weak(?). There are people to ask. There are people who give us orders.

S.FORM: aku mun a setseleɫ a maya, aku mun a seman paravats aravats numa?
S.PHON: aku mun a səʦəɭəʎ a maja, aku mun a səman paravaʦ aravaʦ numa
S.FORM: aku mun a secelelj a maya, aku mun a seman paravac aravac numa?
S.PHON: aku mun a səʦəɭəʎ a maja, aku mun a səman paravaʦ aravaʦ numa
S.TRANSL: Why are you so cold(?)? Why do you flatter(?) so much?

S.FORM: saka qadjaw nimun.
S.PHON: saka qaɉaw nimun.
S.FORM: saka qadjaw nimun.
S.PHON: saka qaɉaw nimun.
S.TRANSL: And it’s up to you.

S.FORM: pai tutsu tja minadjeledjele anga sa kininemneman.
S.PHON: pai tuʦu ca minaɉəɭəɉəɭə aŋa sa kininəmnəman.
S.FORM: pai tucu tja minadjeledjele anga sa kininemneman.
S.PHON: pai tuʦu ʦa minaɉəɭəɉəɭə aŋa sa kininəmnəman.
S.TRANSL: And now we have all cut down our ideas(?).

S.FORM: ɫakua ika mun a masan itaɫ ika mun a masan musaɫ tua nu kininemneman tua nu sinipaqayam.
S.PHON: ʎakua ika mun a masan itaʎ ika mun a masan musaʎ tua nu kininəmnəman tua nu sinipaqajam.
S.FORM: ljakua ika mun a masan italj ika mun a masan musalj tua nu kininemneman tua nu sinipaqayam.
S.PHON: ʎakua ika mun a masan itaʎ ika mun a masan musaʎ tua nu kininəmnəman tua nu sinipaqajam.
S.TRANSL: But you haven’t thought once or twice (?) about what you have put on show.

S.FORM: pai aitsu a keḍikeḍi inika matsidiɫ inika maḍusa matjaɫaɫak, tjuruvu aravats a matjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu a kəɖikəɖi inika maʦidiʎ inika maɖusa macaʎaʎak, curuvu aravaʦ a macaʎaʎak.
S.FORM: pai aicu a kedrikedri inika macidilj inika madrusa matjaljaljak, tjuruvu aravac a matjaljaljak.
S.PHON: pai aiʦu a kəɖikəɖi inika maʦidiʎ inika maɖusa maʦaʎaʎak, ʦuruvu aravaʦ a maʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: This child hasn’t got just one or just two parents; there are very many people (of the parents’ generation)(?).

S.FORM: nu mavadavaday semainu tunazuanga tjara tja nemayain anga nu qempuqepu, tja nemayain anga nu qivu.
S.PHON: nu mavadavadaj səmainu tunazuaŋa cara ca nəmajain aŋa nu qəmpuqəpu, ca nəmajain aŋa nu qivu.
S.FORM: nu mavadavaday semainu tunazuanga tjara tja nemayain anga nu qempuqepu, tja nemayain anga nu qivu.
S.PHON: nu mavadavadaj səmainu tunazuaŋa ʦara ʦa nəmajain aŋa nu qəmpuqəpu, ʦa nəmajain aŋa nu qivu.
S.TRANSL: If they all go off everywhere, what are we to say to call them together again?

S.FORM: nu mateveteveɫ uta tjara liaw aravats a kai, tjara vutselay(?) itjen sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu matəvətəvəʎ uta cara ɭiaw aravaʦ a kai, cara vuʦəɭaj(ʔ) icən sakamaja.
S.FORM: nu matevetevelj uta tjara liaw aravac a kai, tjara vucelay(') itjen sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu matəvətəvəʎ uta ʦara ɭiaw aravaʦ a kai, ʦara vuʦəɭaj(ʔ) iʦən sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: If they are together again, there will be a great deal of talk; we’ll all be pale with shame(?).

S.FORM: saka qadjaw nimun, nu ika mun sa maumaɫ anga nu kininemneman, kitjen a maḍaḍikiḍikitj sakamaya.
S.PHON: saka qaɉaw nimun, nu ika mun sa maumaʎ aŋa nu kininəmnəman, kicən a maɖaɖikiɖikic sakamaja.
S.FORM: saka qadjaw nimun, nu ika mun sa maumalj anga nu kininemneman, kitjen a madradrikidrikitj sakamaya.
S.PHON: saka qaɉaw nimun, nu ika mun sa maumaʎ aŋa nu kininəmnəman, kiʦən a maɖaɖikiɖikiʦ sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: So it’s up to you. If you don’t change your thinking we’ll cut the meeting short.

S.FORM: tjara nasemalimsim mun sakamaya.
S.PHON: cara nasəmaɭimsim mun sakamaja.
S.FORM: tjara nasemalimsim mun sakamaya.
S.PHON: ʦara nasəmaɭimsim mun sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: You are certainly pitiable.

S.FORM: vutselay itjen tua matjaɫaɫak nu sa nu vuvu nu sa nu aɫak.
S.PHON: vuʦəɭaj icən tua macaʎaʎak nu sa nu vuvu nu sa nu aʎak.
S.FORM: vucelay itjen tua matjaljaljak nu sa nu vuvu nu sa nu aljak.
S.PHON: vuʦəɭaj iʦən tua maʦaʎaʎak nu sa nu vuvu nu sa nu aʎak.
S.TRANSL: We are pale with shame before the parents of this your grandchild or child.

S.FORM: sauqaɫay: ui ui, ɫakua nakuya uta mezangal aravats.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: ui ui, ʎakua nakuja uta məzaŋaɭ aravaʦ.
S.FORM: sauqaljay: ui ui, ljakua nakuya uta mezangal aravac.
S.PHON: sauqaʎaj: ui ui, ʎakua nakuja uta məzaŋaɭ aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: Man’s side: Yes, yes. But it’s bad to go on too much.

S.FORM: uɫa men anemaya-i aya men.
S.PHON: uʎa mən anəmaja-i aja mən.
S.FORM: ulja men anemaya-i aya men.
S.PHON: uʎa mən anəmaja-i aja mən.
S.TRANSL: What can be said to us, we ask ourselves.

S.FORM: tjara paquliqulid mun nu pasaqetju mun nu meɫel mun nu kisan kuya mun tusa nia kininemneman tusa separutan tusa ika napenapadjulu, ɫakua aitsu ki nu(?) sipazekatj sakamaya, ki kamseng a nu pinalangedan anga men ta nu varung ta nu kininemneman.
S.PHON: cara paquɭiquɭid mun nu pasaqəcu mun nu məʎəɭ mun nu kisan kuja mun tusa nia kininəmnəman tusa səparutan tusa ika napənapaɉuɭu, ʎakua aiʦu ki nu(ʔ) sipazəkac sakamaja, ki kamsəŋ a nu pinaɭaŋədan aŋa mən ta nu varuŋ ta nu kininəmnəman.
S.FORM: tjara paquliqulid mun nu pasaqetju mun nu meljel mun nu kisan kuya mun tusa nia kininemneman tusa separutan tusa ika napenapadjulu, ljakua aicu ki nu(') sipazekatj sakamaya, ki kamseng a nu pinalangedan anga men ta nu varung ta nu kininemneman.
S.PHON: ʦara paquɭiquɭid mun nu pasaqəʦu mun nu məʎəɭ mun nu kisan kuja mun tusa nia kininəmnəman tusa səparutan tusa ika napənapaɉuɭu, ʎakua aiʦu ki nu(ʔ) sipazəkaʦ sakamaja, ki kamsəŋ a nu pinaɭaŋədan aŋa mən ta nu varuŋ ta nu kininəmnəman.
S.TRANSL: You are quite right when you are harsh with us, when you ? us, when you object to our thinking, to our weakness, to the unsuitability of our provision(?). But you could forgive this(?). It would be enough that you have let us know your feelings and thinking.

S.FORM: manu tiamen uta uɫa tja patjavavavi uɫa tjen a madjadjelidjeli, aya uta nia kininemneman tiamen a sauqaɫay.
S.PHON: manu tiamən uta uʎa ca pacavavavi uʎa cən a maɉaɉəɭiɉəɭi, aja uta nia kininəmnəman tiamən a sauqaʎaj.
S.FORM: manu tiamen uta ulja tja patjavavavi ulja tjen a madjadjelidjeli, aya uta nia kininemneman tiamen a sauqaljay.
S.PHON: manu tiamən uta uʎa ʦa paʦavavavi uʎa ʦən a maɉaɉəɭiɉəɭi, aja uta nia kininəmnəman tiamən a sauqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: We too on the man’s side we want us all to make progress, so that we can all be happy together, that’s our thinking too.

S.FORM: ɫakua saka nu aya itjen a naseparut amen aravats.
S.PHON: ʎakua saka nu aja icən a nasəparut amən aravaʦ.
S.FORM: ljakua saka nu aya itjen a naseparut amen aravac.
S.PHON: ʎakua saka nu aja iʦən a nasəparut amən aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: But what are we to do, as we are so very weak?

S.FORM: qau saka mereanemanga men uta.
S.PHON: qau saka mərəanəmaŋa mən uta.
S.FORM: qau saka mereanemanga men uta.
S.PHON: qau saka mərəanəmaŋa mən uta.
S.TRANSL: And what are we to become too?

S.FORM: qau ika mun a sa salimsim uta tua nu aɫak tua nu vuvu, papaula-u(?) amen uta.
S.PHON: qau ika mun a sa saɭimsim uta tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu, papauɭa-u(ʔ) amən uta.
S.FORM: qau ika mun a sa salimsim uta tua nu aljak tua nu vuvu, papaula-u(') amen uta.
S.PHON: qau ika mun a sa saɭimsim uta tua nu aʎak tua nu vuvu, papauɭa-u(ʔ) amən uta.
S.TRANSL: You don’t take pity either on your child or grandchild. Take pity on us too.

S.FORM: savavayan: ui ɫakua maitazua mun a palalaut tua nu kai, ɫakua ini, tjara kuḍesul amen sakamaya.
S.PHON: savavajan: ui ʎakua maitazua mun a paɭaɭaut tua nu kai, ʎakua ini, cara kuɖəsuɭ amən sakamaja.
S.FORM: savavayan: ui ljakua maitazua mun a palalaut tua nu kai, ljakua ini, tjara kudresul amen sakamaya.
S.PHON: savavajan: ui ʎakua maitazua mun a paɭaɭaut tua nu kai, ʎakua ini, ʦara kuɖəsuɭ amən sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: Woman’s side: Yes, but you keep saying that. But no, we will just stand firm.

S.FORM: aitsu a tja sipatataretarev a kavusaman kadjunangan pana qaɫuqaɫupen vadis, mana tja sipatataretarev numa.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ca sipatatarətarəv a kavusaman kaɉunaŋan pana qaʎuqaʎupən vadis, mana ca sipatatarətarəv numa.
S.FORM: aicu a tja sipatataretarev a kavusaman kadjunangan pana qaljuqaljupen vadis, mana tja sipatataretarev numa.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ʦa sipatatarətarəv a kavusaman kaɉunaŋan pana qaʎuqaʎupən vadis, mana ʦa sipatatarətarəv numa.
S.TRANSL: The bride prices of us real chiefs are land, rivers, hunting grounds, meat tribute, those are our bride prices.

S.FORM: ku pai papatsuni anan amen tua kadjunangan tua pana tua qaɫuqaɫupen tua vadis, sana tje karasurasudj sana tje paɫaɫunguɫungu sana tja qaqivuivu-i a mareka tja kaka a inu tunazuanga a mapuɫat, sana tje, sana tja parepaukuukuz, aya sa kininemneman numa.
S.PHON: ku pai papaʦuni anan amən tua kaɉunaŋan tua pana tua qaʎuqaʎupən tua vadis, sana cə karasurasuɉ sana cə paʎaʎuŋuʎuŋu sana ca qaqivuivu-i a marəka ca kaka a inu tunazuaŋa a mapuʎat, sana cə, sana ca parəpaukuukuz, aja sa kininəmnəman numa.
S.FORM: ku pai papacuni anan amen tua kadjunangan tua pana tua qaljuqaljupen tua vadis, sana tje karasurasudj sana tje paljaljunguljungu sana tja qaqivuivu-i a mareka tja kaka a inu tunazuanga a mapuljat, sana tje, sana tja parepaukuukuz, aya sa kininemneman numa.
S.PHON: ku pai papaʦuni anan amən tua kaɉunaŋan tua pana tua qaʎuqaʎupən tua vadis, sana ʦə karasurasuɉ sana ʦə paʎaʎuŋuʎuŋu sana ʦa qaqivuivu-i a marəka ʦa kaka a inu tunazuaŋa a mapuʎat, sana ʦə, sana ʦa parəpaukuukuz, aja sa kininəmnəman numa.
S.TRANSL: So then show us land, rivers, hunting grounds, meat tribute and we’ll all agree, and we’ll put them side by side, and we’ll tell all our brothers and sisters everywhere, and we’ll-and we’ll have a betrothal, is what we think.

S.FORM: ini nu aya mun, ki tjen a maḍaḍikiḍikitj sakamaya.
S.PHON: ini nu aja mun, ki cən a maɖaɖikiɖikic sakamaja.
S.FORM: ini nu aya mun, ki tjen a madradrikidrikitj sakamaya.
S.PHON: ini nu aja mun, ki ʦən a maɖaɖikiɖikiʦ sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: If you say no, we’ll just cut the talk short.

S.FORM: nu uri puaɫak tiamadju, nu uri tinveteveɫ a zinkatj nua naqemati, kitjen a maumaumaɫ anga nu tjaiviliɫ a mazepzep numa.
S.PHON: nu uri puaʎak tiamaɉu, nu uri tinvətəvəʎ a zinkac nua naqəmati, kicən a maumaumaʎ aŋa nu caiviɭiʎ a mazəpzəp numa.
S.FORM: nu uri pualjak tiamadju, nu uri tinvetevelj a zinkatj nua naqemati, kitjen a maumaumalj anga nu tjaivililj a mazepzep numa.
S.PHON: nu uri puaʎak tiamaɉu, nu uri tinvətəvəʎ a zinkaʦ nua naqəmati, kiʦən a maumaumaʎ aŋa nu ʦaiviɭiʎ a mazəpzəp numa.
S.TRANSL: If they are to have children, if what the creator has provided is to be brought together, we’ll renew the discussion in the future.

S.FORM: aitsu a tja zepzepan tutsu a qezemezemetj tjara qemulip sakamaya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ca zəpzəpan tuʦu a qəzəməzəməc cara qəmuɭip sakamaja.
S.FORM: aicu a tja zepzepan tucu a qezemezemetj tjara qemulip sakamaya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ʦa zəpzəpan tuʦu a qəzəməzəməʦ ʦara qəmuɭip sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: Our discussion tonight is just a waste of time.

S.FORM: sa patjezuanga sa tja zepzepan.
S.PHON: sa pacəzuaŋa sa ca zəpzəpan.
S.FORM: sa patjezuanga sa tja zepzepan.
S.PHON: sa paʦəzuaŋa sa ʦa zəpzəpan.
S.TRANSL: So that’s the end of our discussion.
